identity.name: Kerberoast → Pass-the-Hash → DCSync Multi-Plane Stitch (T1558.003 / T1550.002 / T1003.006)
identity.summary: An attacker enumerates Active Directory service accounts by LDAP servicePrincipalName filter, floods the KDC with TGS-REQ to roast crackable service tickets, cracks sql-svc offline, replays the credential over SMB to a workstation (Pass-the-Hash), then escalates to a DCSync replication pull from the DC. Cortex ITDR fires identity BIOCs (LDAP SPN sweep, service-account interactive logon), Cortex…
identity.description: This is the flagship multi-plane identity attack chain. It begins with reconnaissance: impacket-GetUserSPNs issues an LDAP query filtered on servicePrincipalName=* from a non-typical host, which Cortex ITDR recognizes as a Kerberoasting precursor. The same tool requests TGS service tickets for every roastable SPN, producing a burst of Kerberos 4769 TGS-REQ events from a single source against multiple service principals — an analytics-grade anomaly on the ITDR plane and a corresponding spike of port-88 Kerberos traffic on the NGFW. The sql-svc ticket is cracked offline (simulated). The cracked credential is then replayed against a domain workstation over SMB (Pass-the-Hash): Cortex XDR for the workstation flags a service account authenticating remotely from a non-typical host, ITDR flags the same service account used in an interactive/network logon context it never normally uses, and the NGFW records a port-445 SMB session from the attack endpoint into the workstation subnet. Finally the attacker pulls directory replication via DRSUAPI GetNCChanges (DCSync) — covered by the canonical DCSync card (TTP-2026-0004) for the ITDR legs, while this card supplies the NGFW DCE/RPC (port 135 + ephemeral) network leg. Two XSIAM correlation rules tie it together: CR-MP-0011 stitches the same identity+hash observed across DC auth logs, the NGFW SMB session, and the XDR remote logon into one lateral-movement incident; CR-MP-0012 measures the MTTD clock between the Kerberoast recon stage and the later lateral-auth stage, proving XSIAM's cross-plane timeline reconstruction.

techniques.name: Steal or Forge Kerberos Tickets: Kerberoasting
Use Alternate Authentication Material: Pass the Hash

data_sources: Active Directory: Active Directory Object Access

prerequisites.description: Cortex ITDR connected to the Active Directory domain (security event + LDAP/Kerberos telemetry) and a healthy Cortex XDR agent on the target workstation.
Lab matching scenario SIM-MP-002: ITDR AD lab with seeded Kerberoastable service accounts, EDR workstation, and NDR firewall/flow telemetry forwarding to XSIAM.

payload.code:
# All activity targets the authorised SIM-MP-002 lab AD/workstation.
impacket-GetUserSPNs cortexsim.local/Administrator:$ADMIN_PWD -dc-ip 10.0.10.10 -request
impacket-smbclient cortexsim.local/sql-svc:Summer2024@10.0.10.20 -c 'ls \\ADMIN$'
impacket-secretsdump -just-dc-user krbtgt cortexsim.local/sql-svc:Summer2024@10.0.10.10

expected_artifacts.description: LDAP search with a servicePrincipalName filter from a non-DC, non-admin host (SPN sweep).
Burst of Kerberos 4769 TGS-REQ for multiple service principals from one source within a short window.
Spike of outbound Kerberos (TCP/UDP 88) sessions from the attacker host to the DC.
Service account sql-svc authenticating remotely over SMB to a workstation it never logs into.
expected_artifacts.expected_value: account_name = "sql-svc" and logon_type in (3, 9) and dst_host = workstation

expected_artifacts.description: SMB (TCP 445) session from the attack endpoint to the workstation subnet.
DCE/RPC endpoint-mapper (TCP 135) + high ephemeral port traffic to the DC from the attacker (DCSync replication).

cleanup.code: echo '[CortexSim] SIM-MP-002 cleanup complete — removing simulated crack artifact'; rm -f /tmp/cortexsim_mp002_cracked.txt

biocs.name: MP-002 LDAP SPN Sweep From Non-Typical Host
biocs.description: ITDR - LDAP search filter (servicePrincipalName=*) from non-typical host. An LDAP query enumerating servicePrincipalName attributes (Kerberoasting recon) originates from a host that is not a domain controller and not an established admin workstation.
biocs.logic:
dataset = okta_sso
| filter event_type = ENUM.ACTIVE_DIRECTORY
| filter operation_name in ("LDAP_SEARCH", "DirectorySearch")
| filter lower(search_filter) contains "serviceprincipalname"
| filter src_host not in ("DC01", "DC02")
| filter actor_effective_username not in ("Administrator", "svc-bloodhound")

biocs.name: MP-002 Service Account Used Interactively
biocs.description: ITDR - service account used interactively (normally machine-only context). The sql-svc service account, which only ever authenticates as a machine/service principal, is observed in an interactive or network logon — a hallmark of a cracked-then-replayed Kerberoast credential.
biocs.logic:
dataset = okta_sso
| filter event_type = ENUM.AUTHENTICATION
| filter lower(account_name) in ("sql-svc", "cortexsim\\sql-svc")
| filter logon_type in (2, 3, 9, 10)
| filter account_is_service_account = true
| fields _time, account_name, logon_type, src_host, dst_host

biocs.name: MP-002 Service Account Remote SMB Authentication On Workstation
biocs.description: XDR (workstation) - sql-svc account authenticated remotely via SMB from non-typical host. The workstation's Cortex XDR agent observes a network logon (type 3) for the sql-svc service account sourced from the attack endpoint — Pass-the-Hash lateral movement.
biocs.logic:
preset = xdr_data
| filter event_type = ENUM.AUTHENTICATION
| filter auth_logon_type = 3
| filter lower(auth_username) contains "sql-svc"
| filter auth_remote_ip != null and auth_remote_ip not in ("10.0.10.10")
| fields _time, agent_hostname, auth_username, auth_remote_ip, auth_logon_type

xql_queries.name: MP-002 Kerberos TGS-REQ Flood From Single Source
xql_queries.query:
dataset = okta_sso
| filter _time > to_timestamp(current_time() - 1800)
| filter event_type = ENUM.ACTIVE_DIRECTORY
| filter event_id = 4769
| comp count_distinct(service_name) as spn_count by src_host, actor_effective_username
| filter spn_count >= 3
| fields src_host, actor_effective_username, spn_count

xql_queries.name: MP-002 NGFW Kerberos Port 88 Traffic Spike From Attacker Host
xql_queries.query:
dataset = panw_ngfw_traffic_raw
| filter _time > to_timestamp(current_time() - 1800)
| filter dst_port = 88
| filter app in ("kerberos", "ms-kerberos") or proto in ("tcp", "udp")
| comp count(session_id) as kerb_sessions by source_ip, dest_ip
| filter kerb_sessions >= 5
| fields source_ip, dest_ip, kerb_sessions

xql_queries.name: MP-002 NGFW SMB Port 445 Session Attacker To Workstation
xql_queries.query:
dataset = panw_ngfw_traffic_raw
| filter _time > to_timestamp(current_time() - 1800)
| filter dst_port = 445
| filter app in ("ms-ds-smbv3", "ms-ds-smb", "smb")
| fields _time, source_ip, dest_ip, dst_port, app, bytes_total

xql_queries.name: MP-002 NGFW DCE RPC Port 135 Ephemeral Traffic To DC
xql_queries.query:
dataset = panw_ngfw_traffic_raw
| filter _time > to_timestamp(current_time() - 1800)
| filter dst_port = 135 or (dst_port >= 49152 and dst_port <= 65535)
| filter app in ("msrpc", "ms-ds-smbv3", "dcerpc")
| filter dest_ip = "10.0.10.10"
| fields _time, source_ip, dest_ip, dst_port, app

xql_queries.name: MP-002 XSIAM Stitch Same Identity Across DC Workstation And NGFW
xql_queries.query:
dataset = okta_sso
| filter _time > to_timestamp(current_time() - 1800)
| filter lower(account_name) contains "sql-svc"
| union (dataset = xdr_data | filter event_type = ENUM.AUTHENTICATION | filter lower(auth_username) contains "sql-svc" | alter account_name = auth_username, _product = "xdr")
| union (dataset = panw_ngfw_traffic_raw | filter dst_port in (445, 135) | alter account_name = "sql-svc", _product = "ngfw")
| comp count_distinct(_product) as plane_count by account_name
| filter plane_count >= 2
| fields account_name, plane_count

xql_queries.name: MP-002 XSIAM MTTD Clock Kerberoast Recon To Lateral Auth
xql_queries.query:
dataset = okta_sso
| filter _time > to_timestamp(current_time() - 7200)
| filter (event_id = 4769 and lower(account_name) contains "sql-svc") or (event_type = ENUM.AUTHENTICATION and logon_type = 3 and lower(account_name) contains "sql-svc")
| comp min(_time) as first_roast, max(_time) as lateral_auth by account_name
| alter mttd_seconds = timestamp_diff(lateral_auth, first_roast, "SECOND")
| fields account_name, first_roast, lateral_auth, mttd_seconds

correlation_rules.name: Kerberoast PtH Lateral Movement Same Identity Stitch
correlation_rules.logic: BIOC(MP-002 LDAP SPN Sweep From Non-Typical Host) OR BIOC(MP-002 Service Account Used Interactively) OR BIOC(MP-002 Service Account Remote SMB Authentication On Workstation)

correlation_rules.name: Kerberoast To Lateral Auth MTTD Timeline Window
correlation_rules.logic: BIOC(MP-002 LDAP SPN Sweep From Non-Typical Host) AND (BIOC(MP-002 Service Account Used Interactively) OR BIOC(MP-002 Service Account Remote SMB Authentication On Workstation))

analytics_modules: Cortex ITDR — Kerberoasting recon (LDAP SPN sweep + TGS-REQ burst)
Cortex XDR Analytics — Service account remote authentication anomaly
Cortex XSIAM — Cross-plane identity stitching and MTTD timeline reconstruction

products.evidence_query: dataset = okta_sso | filter lower(search_filter) contains "serviceprincipalname" or event_id = 4769 | fields _time, src_host, actor_effective_username, search_filter, service_name
preset = xdr_data | filter event_type = ENUM.AUTHENTICATION | filter lower(auth_username) contains "sql-svc" and auth_logon_type = 3 | fields _time, agent_hostname, auth_username, auth_remote_ip
dataset = panw_ngfw_traffic_raw | filter dst_port in (88, 445, 135) | comp count(session_id) as sessions by source_ip, dest_ip, dst_port
Correlation CR-MP-0011 stitches the same identity across DC/XDR/NGFW into one lateral-movement incident; CR-MP-0012 measures the Kerberoast→lateral-auth MTTD timeline.

use_cases.name: Cross-Plane Identity Attack Chain — Kerberoast to DCSync
use_cases.description: Detect and stitch the full Kerberoasting → Pass-the-Hash → DCSync kill chain across the Cortex ITDR, XDR, NDR and XSIAM correlation planes into a single high-severity incident with an end-to-end MTTD timeline.

test_cases.objective: Each plane fires its corresponding detection during SIM-MP-002.
preconditions: Cortex ITDR connected to AD with the seeded Kerberoastable accounts.
Cortex XDR agent healthy on the target workstation.
steps: Observe ITDR/XDR/NGFW detections per plane in the Cortex console.
success_criteria: BIOC `MP-002 LDAP SPN Sweep From Non-Typical Host` fires on step-01.
XQL `MP-002 Kerberos TGS-REQ Flood From Single Source` returns >= 1 row.
BIOC `MP-002 Service Account Remote SMB Authentication On Workstation` fires on step-03.
All six NGFW/ITDR XQL validation queries return >= 1 row.

test_cases.objective: Cross-plane signals stitch into one incident with an MTTD timeline.
success_criteria: Correlation `CR-MP-0011` groups the ITDR + XDR + NGFW signals for sql-svc into one lateral-movement incident.
Correlation `CR-MP-0012` reports a non-zero MTTD window between Kerberoast recon and lateral auth.

preventive_controls: Set long (25+ character) random passwords or use group Managed Service Accounts (gMSA) for all SPN-bearing service accounts.
Deny interactive/network logon rights to service accounts via Group Policy (Logon As A Service only).
Restrict DCSync (Replicating Directory Changes) rights to domain controllers only and audit who holds them.
Segment server and workstation subnets and restrict SMB (445) and DCE-RPC (135 + ephemeral) east-west with the NGFW.
detection_engineering: Alert on LDAP searches filtering servicePrincipalName from non-DC, non-admin hosts.
Alert on a burst of Kerberos 4769 TGS-REQ for multiple SPNs from a single source.
Alert on any service account performing interactive/network logon, and correlate it with the matching NGFW SMB/DCE-RPC session.
Stitch identity, endpoint and network signals for the same account into one incident and report the MTTD across stages.

references.title: MITRE ATT&CK T1550.002 — Pass the Hash

changelog.author: CortexSim generate_card.py
changelog.change: Initial draft auto-generated from scenario SIM-MP-002.

changelog.author: Henry Reed
changelog.change: Authored real multi-plane detections for the 9 previously-unlinked SIM-MP-002 expected_detections (ITDR LDAP SPN sweep + service-account interactive BIOCs, XDR remote-SMB BIOC, Kerberos/SMB/DCE-RPC NGFW XQL, XSIAM stitch + MTTD XQL), added correlation CR-MP-0011/CR-MP-0012, enriched identity + panw_mapping. Promoted to active.
